selection_criteria: small and safe — docs-only, checker-only, test-only, or CI-only
backward compatible — no API or CLI contract changes
no runtime trading impact — no broker, execution, safety, risk, or provider default changes
patch scope per Semantic Versioning 2.0.0 — backward-compatible bug fixes or small maintenance only
verified by evidence — reproducible from repo state, CI logs, or checker output
testable — no network or credentials required

candidates.title: main_health.py drift prevention after release cutovers
candidates.source: scripts/main_health.py hardcodes EXPECTED_SOURCE_VERSION, PUBLIC_RELEASE, and NEXT_UNREQUESTED_RELEASE_TAG; manual updates are recurring post-release friction
candidates.acceptance_criteria: Constants centralized in ReleaseMetadata; validation detects drift against git tags and source version; tests verify detection. Implemented in Batch 60.
candidates.reason: Reduces recurring manual drift and post-release test breakage.

candidates.title: Release-assurance artifact hygiene follow-up
candidates.source: scripts/release_assurance.py produces untracked local artifacts under artifacts/release_assurance/ that require manual backup before push
candidates.acceptance_criteria: Generated artifact checker provides clearer guidance on backing up vs. removing local release-assurance artifacts; docs reference the hygiene workflow consistently. Implemented in Batch 62.
candidates.reason: Keeps local worktree predictable and post-push checks clean.

candidates.title: Package distribution checker follow-up after v0.6.3
candidates.source: scripts/check_package_distribution.py was hotfixed in v0.6.3; --dry-run and missing-tool paths can be further hardened
candidates.acceptance_criteria: --dry-run produces a deterministic plan report; missing tooling yields actionable hints instead of tracebacks. Implemented in Batch 61.
candidates.reason: Hardens a checker that was part of the v0.6.3 release rationale.

candidates.title: Release notes / trust center consistency after v0.6.3
candidates.source: Batch 58 updated most docs to v0.6.3; a final pass can align remaining examples, smoke commands, and cross-references
candidates.acceptance_criteria: All public-release references in docs and checkers resolve to v0.6.3; stale v0.6.2 examples are removed.
candidates.reason: Closes the post-release docs polish loop.

candidates.title: Long-running check timeout documentation refinement
candidates.source: scripts/check_runtime_diagnostics.py and checks-reference.md already document timeout triage sufficiently
candidates.acceptance_criteria: Add slow-machine estimates for research_check.sh and full_test gates without weakening timeouts.
candidates.reason: Already sufficient; defer unless operator feedback indicates confusion.

candidates.title: CI deterministic test reliability follow-up
candidates.source: Historical scheduled Full Test failures and timing-sensitive tests benefit from better isolation and deterministic ordering
candidates.acceptance_criteria: Timing-sensitive tests mock time or filesystem ordering; JSON diff comparisons use sorted keys where appropriate.
candidates.reason: Improves CI signal-to-noise without adding retries or skips.

candidates.title: Generated artifact cleanup ergonomics
candidates.source: Operators manually back up generated artifacts; checker messages could be more actionable
candidates.acceptance_criteria: Checker emits actionable backup commands; no destructive cleanup is performed automatically. Implemented in Batch 61.
candidates.reason: Reduces operator friction during post-push hygiene.

candidates.title: Public docs consistency checker expansion
candidates.source: scripts/check_public_docs_consistency.py could validate README status line and orphaned release notes
candidates.acceptance_criteria: Checker validates README status line matches current public release; warns on orphaned release notes.
candidates.reason: Valuable but broader than a minimal patch; defer to a dedicated docs-checker batch.

candidates.title: PyPI non-publish messaging consistency
candidates.source: Release notes use slightly different phrasing for PyPI not published
candidates.acceptance_criteria: All PyPI references use consistent negated phrasing; no claim of publication exists. Implemented in Batch 62.
candidates.reason: Low-cost clarity improvement that reinforces the no-publish policy.

candidates.title: v0.6.4 release prep checker planning
candidates.source: Pattern established by check_v061_release_prep.py, check_v062_release_prep.py, and check_v063_release_prep.py
candidates.acceptance_criteria: Skeleton validates absence of docs/releases/v0.6.4.md and 0.6.4 version bump in planning mode; full release-prep mode activates after version bump.
candidates.reason: Prepares checker infrastructure without enabling it prematurely.

deferred.title: Long-running check timeout documentation refinement
deferred.reason: Already documented sufficiently. Defer until operator feedback indicates confusion.

deferred.title: Public docs consistency checker expansion
deferred.reason: Valuable but broader than a minimal patch. Defer to a dedicated docs-checker batch.

deferred.title: New broker adapters (Binance completion, CCXT enablement, IBKR implementation)
deferred.reason: New features, not bug fixes. Defer to v0.7.0 or later minor release.

deferred.title: New strategy packs or backtest engine expansion
deferred.reason: New features out of patch scope. Defer to v0.7.0 or later.

deferred.title: Dashboard rewrite or new UI capabilities
deferred.reason: Out of patch scope. Requires design review.

deferred.title: Major architecture refactor
deferred.reason: Out of patch scope. Too risky for a patch release.

rejected.title: Live trading default changes
rejected.reason: Atlas remains disabled-by-default for live trading.

rejected.title: Live submit enablement
rejected.reason: Live submit remains disabled by default.

rejected.title: Broker execution enablement
rejected.reason: No broker adapter may be promoted to default live-submit enabled.

rejected.title: Provider execution unlock
rejected.reason: Provider adapters remain fail-closed and disabled by default.

rejected.title: Automatic skill activation
rejected.reason: Skill candidates remain manually reviewed and offline.

rejected.title: Automatic learning execution
rejected.reason: Learning workflows remain offline and manually triggered.

rejected.title: PyPI publish
rejected.reason: Not approved in this planning batch; requires separate process.

rejected.title: Large architecture rewrite
rejected.reason: Out of patch scope and too risky.

safety_boundaries: src/atlas_agent/brokers/ — no new submit paths, no execution enabled by default
src/atlas_agent/risk/ — risk limits must not be weakened
Live trading remains disabled by default
Live submit remains disabled by default
Provider execution remains disabled by default
Broker execution remains disabled by default
Human approval remains required for live order flow
Credentials are not loaded unless explicitly configured
No provider APIs, broker APIs, or real webhooks are called from CI or local checks
Version is not bumped to 0.6.4 in this batch
No tag, GitHub release, or PyPI publish yet
non_goals: No tag or GitHub release creation
No broker, provider, risk, approval, or kill-switch behavior changes
